topics.title: Div & Span
topics.body: `<div>` is a block-level container for grouping content and creating page sections. `<span>` is an inline container for wrapping text or other inline elements without breaking the flow. Use `<div>` for structural layout and `<span>` for styling or scripting small inline portions.

topics.title: Headings
topics.body: HTML provides six heading levels: `<h1>` through `<h6>`. `<h1>` is the top-level heading (use only one per page), and `<h6>` is the least important. Headings create a document outline and improve accessibility for screen readers.

topics.title: Links
topics.body: The `<a>` (anchor) element creates hyperlinks. The `href` attribute specifies the URL. Use `target="_blank"` to open in a new tab (pair with `rel="noopener noreferrer"` for security). Links can wrap text, images, or block elements.

topics.title: Images
topics.body: The `` element embeds images. Always include the `alt` attribute for accessibility. Use `loading="lazy"` to defer off-screen images. Specify `width` and `height` to prevent layout shift during loading.

topics.title: Tables
topics.body: Tables use `<table>`, `<tr>` (rows), `<td>` (data cells), and `<th>` (header cells). Wrap header rows in `<thead>` and body rows in `<tbody>` for semantic clarity. Use `<caption>` for an accessible table title.
topics.exampleHTML:
Name Role
AdaEngineer
BoDesigner

topics.title: Forms
topics.body: Forms collect user input with `<form>`. Common controls: `<input>` (text, email, checkbox, radio), `<button>`, `<select>` with `<option>`, and `<textarea>`. Use `<label>` with `for` attribute to link labels to inputs for accessibility.
topics.exampleHTML:
<form style="font-size:13px">
  <label style="display:block;margin-bottom:2px;font-weight:600">Name</label>
  <input type="text" placeholder="Enter name" style="padding:6px;border:1px solid #cbd5e1;border-radius:4px;width:200px">
  <button type="button" style="margin-top:8px;padding:6px 14px;background:#2563eb;color:white;border:none;border-radius:4px;cursor:default">Submit</button>
</form>

topics.title: Lists
topics.body: Unordered lists (`<ul>`) use bullet points. Ordered lists (`<ol>`) use numbers. Each item is an `<li>`. Definition lists (`<dl>`) pair `<dt>` terms with `<dd>` descriptions. Nest lists for hierarchical structures.
topics.exampleHTML:
• First bullet point
  • Second bullet point
    • Nested item A
    • Nested item B

topics.title: Class & ID
topics.body: `class` targets multiple elements with shared styles (reusable). `id` uniquely identifies a single element — use for anchors, JavaScript targeting, or specific style overrides. IDs have higher CSS specificity than classes.
topics.exampleHTML:
id: unique-card
class: card rounded

topics.title: Style Attribute
topics.body: The `style` attribute applies inline CSS directly to an element. Use sparingly — prefer external stylesheets or `<style>` blocks. Inline styles are useful for dynamic values or quick prototyping but hurt maintainability at scale.

topics.title: Data Attributes
topics.body: `data-*` attributes store custom data on any HTML element. They are ignored by browsers but accessible via JavaScript (`element.dataset`). Use them to embed application state, configuration values, or metadata without polluting other attributes.

topics.title: Click & Events
topics.body: Event attributes like `onclick`, `onmouseover`, `onchange`, and `onsubmit` attach JavaScript handlers directly to elements. Modern practice prefers `addEventListener` in scripts, but inline handlers remain common for quick prototyping.

topics.title: Best Practices
topics.body: Write semantic HTML: use elements for their intended purpose (`<nav>` for navigation, `<main>` for content, `<article>` for self-contained pieces). Always include `lang` on `<html>`, use `alt` on images, and ensure keyboard navigability. Validate your markup with the W3C validator.

Page Title

Main content area.
